summary: CC-0092 replaces Owns(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}) with an explicit Watches(...) using pushSecretToKeystoneMapper (namespace-scoped List + name-match against openBaoBackupPushSecretNames, List-error log-and-swallow, dedup via map[types.NamespacedName]struct{}) and pushSecretRelevantChangePredicate (admits Create/Delete/Generic unconditionally; admits Update iff finalizers-as-set, DeletionTimestamp presence, or Generation differs; suppresses status-only ESO ticks). Seven mapper unit tests and six predicate unit tests are added in keystone_controller_test.go; reconcile_secrets.go / reconcile_secrets_test.go / integration_test.go are untouched. Docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md gains a PushSecret row in the Watches table and a new 'PushSecret Name-Match Mapper (CC-0092)' subsection cross-referencing the OpenBao Finalizer Pass-0/Pass-1 state machine.

tests_checklist.item: All TestPushSecretToKeystoneMapper_* and TestPushSecretPredicate_* unit tests pass under `go test -race` (verified: ok 1.612s)
Regression suite TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets|TestReconcileDeleteOpenBao|TestSecretToKeystoneMapper passes without source edits to reconcile_secrets.go (verified: ok 0.145s)

Edge cases covered: fernet backup match, credential backup match, unrelated name skip, multi-keystone dedup, empty namespace, cross-namespace skip, list-error swallow, finalizer add, finalizer remove, DeletionTimestamp flip, status-only suppression, Create/Delete/Generic admit, Generation change
Cross-namespace test uses recordingListInterceptor to pin client.InNamespace(obj.Namespace) (verifies the blocking criterion REQ-002 mechanically)
Predicate tests construct event.UpdateEvent/CreateEvent/DeleteEvent directly — no fake client required
reconcile_secrets.go, reconcile_secrets_test.go, integration_test.go unchanged in diff (verified: `git diff --stat main...HEAD --` for those paths returned empty)

Envtest integration tests (TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption, TestIntegration_FinalizerLifecycle_AddAndRemove, TestIntegration_FinalizerBrownfieldDeletion) not run in this sandbox (no KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS); criterion requires that they pass without assertion edits — the integration_test.go file is unchanged which satisfies the 'no assertion edits' half, runtime convergence must be confirmed in CI

code_quality_checklist.item: Mirrors structure of existing mapper helpers (secretToKeystoneMapper, mariaDBToKeystoneMapper, clusterSecretStoreToKeystoneMapper) in the same file
Consistent log-and-swallow List-error handling matching sibling mappers
Uses openBaoBackupPushSecretNames as single source of truth for backup name set (no duplicated string literals)
Uses stdlib slices.Clone/Sort/Equal (Go 1.21+) rather than manual loops for finalizersEqualAsSet
No dead code, no TODO comments, no commented-out alternatives
CC-0092 feature ID present on every new doc comment block, predicate, mapper, helper, and test (FEATURE_ID_REQUIRED rule 11)
go build / go vet clean on operators/keystone/...
golangci-lint reports only pre-existing issues in unchanged files (rotation_validation.go:58 errorlint, reconcile_credential_test.go:1059 SA1019); none in the CC-0092 diff

security_checklist.item: No user input handled — mapper and predicate operate on controller-runtime event objects only
No hardcoded secrets, no path traversal surface, no SQL
List is namespace-scoped (client.InNamespace) — no privilege escalation via cluster-wide enumeration; workqueue cannot be poisoned from a different tenant namespace
Predicate Update nil-guards ObjectOld/ObjectNew (returns true conservatively), avoiding a nil-deref DoS on malformed events
No sensitive data in logs (only 'listing Keystone CRs for PushSecret watch' + err)

architecture_checklist.item: Replaces Owns(PushSecret) with Watches(...) + predicate — exactly one registration for esov1alpha1.PushSecret in SetupWithManager (REQ-001)
No field indexer added — the backup name set is bounded (2) and derivable from keystone.Name, so an in-memory compare is the right call (matches review criterion and CC-0087 decision boundary)
Predicate centralises event filtering; mapper centralises name matching — clean separation of concerns

Owner-ref inspection is deliberately omitted per doc comment: the backup PushSecrets have a controller reference set by EnsurePushSecret, but Owns(...)+Watches(...) on the same GVK would double-enqueue. Name-based mapping is the single source of truth — explicitly documented

Watch handler filter matches actual ownership model (review pattern 'watch-handler-filter-must-match-actual-ownership-model-of-watched-resources'): backup PushSecrets have ownerReferences pointing to Keystone via EnsurePushSecret's controller reference, but the mapper is name-based and does not depend on owner-ref, so the watch works whether or not the controller reference is present

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Reuses openBaoBackupPushSecretNames instead of duplicating the backup-name derivation
Reuses recordingListInterceptor / newMapperFakeClient / newMapperFakeClientBuilder (same helpers used by TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_*); review criterion requires this for user story 5
No speculative generality — predicate admits exactly the three transitions Pass-0/Pass-1 branch on, nothing more

fail_fast_checklist.item: List error is logged-and-swallowed per handler.MapFunc contract (no error return) — consistent with all sibling mappers
Predicate Update nil-check on ObjectOld/ObjectNew returns true (conservative admit) — avoids silently dropping a malformed event

defensive_checklist.item: finalizersEqualAsSet clones inputs via slices.Clone before sorting — no mutation of caller-owned finalizer slices from GetFinalizers()
Dedup via map[types.NamespacedName]struct{} guards against the (unlikely) case where both backup names collide for different Keystones

suggested_improvements: TestPushSecretPredicate_FinalizerRemoveAdmitted hard-codes the literal 'external-secrets.io/cleanup' because the existing esoCleanupFinalizer const lives behind //go:build integration. Consider promoting the const to an unexported package-level constant in reconcile_secrets.go (alongside esoPushSecretFinalizer) so both unit and integration tests can reference a single source of truth. Low priority; the inline comment already explains the reason.

The mapper's doc comment says 'enqueue exactly once' and implements dedup via map[types.NamespacedName]struct{}. In practice a PushSecret name uniquely identifies at most one Keystone (because backup names are '<ks.Name>-fernet-keys-backup' / '<ks.Name>-credential-keys-backup'), so len(seen) is always 0 or 1. The dedup is defensive, harmless, and symmetric with secretToKeystoneMapper — worth keeping, but the rationale could note that dedup is a future-proofing safety net rather than a current-behaviour requirement.

Consider adding a brief note in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md's 'PushSecret Name-Match Mapper' section on why Owns(PushSecret) was rejected in this iteration (status-only ESO ticks flooding the workqueue) — the code comment captures this but the docs skip that motivation.
next_steps: Confirm in CI that TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption, TestIntegration_FinalizerLifecycle_AddAndRemove, and TestIntegration_FinalizerBrownfieldDeletion still pass and each Eventually(finalizer-wait) cycle converges in <5s (acceptance criterion in the feature description) — sandbox has no envtest binaries so this must be verified in the regular CI pipeline.
